laws.title: Public Law 19-145 | Military Officer Accountability Act (MOAA)
laws.final_statutory_text: Replaces 10 U.S.C. 1161 and 10 U.S.C. 1181, 1182(a), 1184, 1185(a)(1), 1186(a), and 1187(b) with revised officer show-cause and removal rules.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The supplied source is usable and no later law in the reviewed set repeals PL 19-145 itself.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct replacement of seven Title 10 provisions; no separate transfer or redesignation chain was resolved.
laws.status_basis: The act is intact and operative. It expressly repeals and replaces seven Title 10 provisions governing officer show-cause and removal procedures, and nothing in the reviewed material repeals PL 19-145 itself.

current_implementation.detail: The Title 10 treatment reflects the enacted amendments rather than a bare history note.

recommended_actions: Keep the Title 10 amendments aligned with the enacted text.
Preserve the replacement structure for the seven affected provisions.

laws.title: Public Law 19-146 | Refurbishing the Military Legal System Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Source unavailable; no operative statutory text can be verified.
laws.chronology: Source-limited. The accessible file is a Google Drive permission-denial page, so no lawful chronology can be fixed from the text itself.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No determinable repeal, transfer, or redesignation chain could be resolved from the available source.
laws.status_basis: The only accessible source is unrecoverable permission-denial content, so no operative conclusion about the act can be supported.

current_implementation.detail: The existing XML treats the act as repealed or superseded, but the source does not support that certainty.

recommended_actions: Retain only as a source-limited historical note.
Remove boilerplate certainty about repeal or supersession.
Do not invent operative text.

laws.title: Public Law 19-147 | Diplomatic Standards Act (DSA)
laws.final_statutory_text: Restates a diplomatic-relations framework and repeals 8 U.S.C. 1102; the act is later repealed by PL 31-227 without revival of the section.
laws.chronology: Current at enactment, but later law PL 31-227 explicitly repeals PL 19-147 and does not restore 8 U.S.C. 1102.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct repeal of 8 U.S.C. 1102, followed by later repeal of the public law itself; no surviving transfer or redesignation chain was resolved.
laws.status_basis: PL 31-227 later repeals PL 19-147 expressly, so the act survives only as statutory history.

current_implementation.detail: The Title 8 note is the right history-only treatment because the enacted repeal chain is tied to 8 U.S.C. 1102.

recommended_actions: Keep the history note tied to the 8 U.S.C. 1102 reservation and the PL 31-227 repealer chain.
Do not reinstate operative text.

laws.title: Public Law 19-148 | Badges and Entrapment Act (BEA)
laws.final_statutory_text: Creates badge-display rules, defines entrapment, and sets related enforcement consequences, but cites no direct Code host for a text amendment.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is usable and no later law in the reviewed material repeals PL 19-148 itself.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No Code repeal or transfer chain was resolved; the act is properly handled as a statutory note.
laws.status_basis: The act is a generally applicable and permanent enactment, and nothing in the reviewed set repeals it.

current_implementation.detail: The note-only treatment in Title 50 is a reasonable home for the statute, and no new Code section is required.

recommended_actions: Keep the note-only treatment.
Do not invent a positive-law Code section for the act.

laws.title: Public Law 19-149 | Criminal Justice Reform Act (CJRA)
laws.final_statutory_text: Amends 18 U.S.C. 3156(a)(3) and proposes a broad title-wide sentencing-cap regime, but the cap provisions in sections 7 and 8 remain unresolved.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. Section 5 is codifiable now, while the broader sentencing-cap provisions were left unresolved because the source does not safely resolve the drafting choice.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Section 5 is a direct amendment to 18 U.S.C. 3156; sections 7 and 8 would require a separate title-wide sentencing-cap decision.
laws.status_basis: No later repeal of PL 19-149 itself was identified, and the act still contains a direct amendment to 18 U.S.C. 3156(a)(3).

current_implementation.detail: The 18 U.S.C. 3156 amendment is applied, but the title-wide sentencing-cap provisions remain held for review rather than forced into text.

recommended_actions: Keep the 18 U.S.C. 3156 amendment in place.
Leave sections 7 and 8 held until a human interpretation is chosen.
Do not guess at a title-wide rewrite.

laws.title: Public Law 19-150 | Small Businesses Empowerment Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Creates a small-business recognition and support program and a related employment initiative within the Department of Commerce and Labor; no direct Code amendment host is cited.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is usable and no later law in the reviewed set repeals PL 19-150 itself.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No Code repeal or transfer chain was resolved; the act is properly handled as a statutory note.
laws.status_basis: The act is a generally applicable permanent enactment with no cited USC host and no identified repeal.

current_implementation.detail: The Title 15 note placement is a plausible statutory-note anchor for the small-business program.

recommended_actions: Keep the note-only treatment.
Do not invent a positive-law Code section for the act.

laws.title: Public Law 19-151 | Court Fees Prohibition and Protection Act (CTPPA)
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals nineteen Title 28 fee, cost, and deposit provisions, plus 28 U.S.C. 1918(b), and states a general prohibition on court fees outside criminal punishment or contempt.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The direct Title 28 repeals are codifiable, and no later repeal of PL 19-151 itself was identified.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct repeal of twenty Title 28 targets and one subsection; section 5 is a freestanding fee rule without a specific USC host.
laws.status_basis: The act remains operative and expressly repeals multiple Title 28 provisions; nothing in the reviewed set repeals PL 19-151 itself.

current_implementation.detail: The Title 28 repeal history is reflected, and section 5 remains properly handled as a note rather than fabricated positive-law text.

recommended_actions: Keep the Title 28 repeal/reservation chain aligned with the historical note.
Preserve section 5 as a note-style prohibition.

laws.title: Public Law 19-152 | The Commerce Legislation and Labor Aggravations Reformations (CLEAR)
laws.final_statutory_text: Amends 5 U.S.C. 101 and also repeals or rewrites related standalone public-law provisions that are outside the direct USC codification pass.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is usable, no later repeal of PL 19-152 itself was identified, and section 8 is the only direct USC amendment in the act.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct amendment of 5 U.S.C. 101; other sections amend standalone public laws and are not direct USC codification targets.
laws.status_basis: The act is still operative as a general and permanent enactment, and no specific repealer of PL 19-152 was found.

current_implementation.detail: The Title 5 amendment is the correct codification target; the non-USC repeals are outside this direct amendment pass.

recommended_actions: Keep the 5 U.S.C. 101 amendment aligned with the later-law sequence.
Leave the standalone public-law repeals to the non-USC law history.

laws.title: Public Law 22-153 | Fire Department Reform and Nurturing (FDRN)
laws.final_statutory_text: Reorganizes the District of Columbia fire and emergency medical services, defines duties and reporting, and sets home-rule contingencies, but cites no direct USC amendment host.
